decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.3°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized temperature gradient under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
